title: Verify a Manually Removed Tote Is Returned With the Same Tote ID to Its Original Sorter Position
summary: Verify that a tote manually removed from the sorter stand is returned with the same Tote ID to its original sorter position so the system maintains the correct tote-to-location association and avoids operational errors.
when_to_use: Use this procedure during sorter manual tote handling when a tote has been manually removed from the sorter stand and must be checked before or during return to service. This applies to manual operations at the sorter supported by the Zebra scanner, including tote replacement, maintenance, repair, and damaged tote handling contexts described by the source.
not_for: Do not use this as a full tote initialization procedure.
Do not use this as a substitute for rescanning when the same Tote ID is not returned to the original position.
Do not use this procedure to define corrective actions beyond the documented rescanning requirement.

responsibilities: Identify the manually removed tote and its original sorter position.
Check Tote ID information using supported scanner or visible Tote ID information from the source context.
Verify the tote returned matches the original Tote ID and original position.
Escalate or stop normal return flow if the Tote ID or position does not match and rescanning is required.

safety_notes: The source frames this as a support-safe verification activity during manual sorter tote management.
Do not invent additional corrective handling beyond the documented rescanning requirement if Tote ID and position association is not preserved.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the tote being removed and returned

access_required: Access to the tote being removed and returned
Access to Zebra scanner-supported sorter tote management process
system_preconditions: A tote has been manually removed from the sorter stand.
The original sorter position of the removed tote is known or can be identified before return.
Tote ID information can be checked using the documented scanner process or visible Tote ID information supported by the source.

visual_references.description: Sorter scanner operation image tied to page 93 instructions for Add Tote, tote barcode scan, Tote ID verification, and Confirm & Execute in manual sorter tote handling.
Tote initialization procedure image showing sorter stand position barcode and tote barcode scanning context relevant to rescanning association when required.

steps.title: Identify the manually removed tote
steps.purpose: Establish which tote was removed and which sorter position must be preserved.
steps.instruction: Identify the tote that was manually removed from the sorter stand and determine the original sorter position from which it was removed before returning it.
steps.expected_result: The removed tote and its original sorter position are clearly identified.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote to be returned is known and the original sorter position is known.
steps.failure_condition: The removed tote or its original sorter position cannot be confidently identified.

actions.label: Identify removed tote and position
actions.exact_action: Identify the tote that was manually removed from the sorter stand.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Sorter scanner operation context for manual tote handling at the sorter.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the original sorter position cannot be identified.
Escalate if there is uncertainty about which tote was removed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When a tote is manually removed from the sorter stand, it is essential that the tote with the same Tote ID is returned to its original position.
A manually removed tote must be returned with the same Tote ID to its original position on the sorter stand so the system maintains the correct tote-location association.

steps.title: Check the Tote ID of the tote being returned
steps.purpose: Confirm the Tote ID of the tote being returned using source-supported identification methods.
steps.instruction: Check the Tote ID of the tote being returned using the documented scanner process or visible Tote ID information supported by the source. Where the scanner workflow is used, verify the Tote ID is displayed correctly.
steps.expected_result: The Tote ID of the tote being returned is available for comparison.
steps.healthy_condition: The Tote ID can be read or verified correctly through the supported process.
steps.failure_condition: The Tote ID cannot be verified or is not displayed/read correctly.

actions.label: Check Tote ID
actions.exact_action: Check the Tote ID of the tote being returned using the documented scanner process or visible Tote ID information supported by the source.

actions.label: Verify displayed Tote ID
actions.exact_action: Verify the tote ID is displayed correctly.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Add Tote screen context, tote barcode scan, and Tote ID verification on page 93.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the Tote ID cannot be verified.
Escalate if the scanner process does not provide a readable or correct Tote ID.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Nearby instructions say to navigate to the Add Tote screen, scan the tote barcode, verify the tote ID is displayed correctly, and press Confirm & Execute so the AGV takes the tote back into the system.
The Zebra scanner is a versatile hand-held device used at the hospital and the sorter. This section focuses on usage of the scanner at the sorter for managing totes during manual operations.

steps.title: Verify the returned tote has the same Tote ID
steps.purpose: Ensure the tote being returned matches the Tote ID of the tote that was removed.
steps.instruction: Verify that the tote being returned has the same Tote ID as the tote that was removed.
steps.expected_result: The Tote ID of the returned tote matches the Tote ID of the removed tote.
steps.failure_condition: The Tote ID does not match the removed tote's Tote ID.

actions.label: Compare Tote IDs
actions.exact_action: Verify that the tote being returned has the same Tote ID as the tote that was removed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Tote ID information used to confirm the returned tote matches the removed tote.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the Tote ID does not match.
Rescanning is required if the same Tote ID is not returned to the original position.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When a tote is manually removed from the sorter stand, it is essential that the tote with the same Tote ID is returned to its original position.

steps.title: Verify the tote is returned to the original sorter position
steps.purpose: Preserve the original tote-to-location association in the system.
steps.instruction: Verify that the tote is being returned to its original position on the sorter stand.
steps.expected_result: The tote is aligned with the same sorter position from which it was removed.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote is returned to the original sorter position.
steps.failure_condition: The tote is being returned to a different sorter position.

actions.label: Check return position
actions.exact_action: Verify that the tote is being returned to its original position on the sorter stand.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Sorter stand position barcode scanning context relevant to position association and rescanning if needed.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the tote is not being returned to the original position.
Rescanning is required if the tote cannot be returned with the same Tote ID to the original position.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When a tote is manually removed from the sorter stand, it is essential that the tote with the same Tote ID is returned to its original position.

The operator opens the application, selects "Sorter Destinations," scans the tote sorter stand position barcode located in the center of the rack on the top rail, and scans the tote barcode for the tote being placed in the stand.

steps.title: Confirm tote-to-location association is preserved
steps.purpose: Complete the verification that correct system association is maintained and operational errors are avoided.
steps.instruction: Confirm that the tote-to-location association is preserved by returning the same Tote ID to the original position. If this condition is not met, use rescanning as required by the source to properly associate the tote with its designated location.
steps.expected_result: The system association is preserved because the same Tote ID has been returned to the original position, or rescanning is identified as required.
steps.healthy_condition: The same Tote ID is returned to the original position and no reassociation issue is indicated.
steps.failure_condition: The Tote ID and original position association is not preserved, creating a need for rescanning to properly associate the tote with its designated location.

actions.label: Confirm association preserved
actions.exact_action: Confirm that the tote-to-location association is preserved by returning the same Tote ID to the original position.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Manual sorter scanner operation context stating same Tote ID must return to original position and that proper scanning avoids operational errors.
Rescanning context for pairing sorter position barcode and tote barcode when reassociation is required.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop normal return flow if the same Tote ID is not returned to the original position.
Use rescanning if the tote must be properly associated with its designated location.
Escalate if rescanning cannot be completed or association remains uncertain.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Proper scanning ensures that the system recognizes the correct placement and avoids operational errors.
If a tote is manually removed from the sorter stand, the tote with the same Tote ID must be returned to its original position or rescanned to properly associate it with the designated location.
A successful scan pairing causes the scanner to flash green, while an unsuccessful pairing causes a yellow flash and requires rescanning.

success_criteria: The manually removed tote is returned with the same Tote ID.
The tote is returned to its original sorter position.
Operational errors related to incorrect placement are avoided.
healthy_conditions: The Tote ID of the returned tote matches the removed tote.
The tote is returned to the original sorter stand position.
Proper scanning confirms correct placement when scanner workflow is used.
If rescanning is required, scan pairing succeeds.
failure_conditions: The returned tote does not have the same Tote ID as the removed tote.
The tote is returned to a different sorter position.
The tote-to-location association is not preserved.
The Tote ID cannot be verified through the supported process.
Rescanning is required because correct association was not maintained.
escalation_guidance: If the same Tote ID is not returned to the original position, rescanning is required to properly associate the tote with its designated location.
Do not apply undocumented corrective actions beyond the rescanning requirement from the source.
Escalate when the removed tote, original position, or resulting association cannot be confidently verified.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Sorter scanner operation image for Add Tote, tote barcode scan, Tote ID verification, and manual tote return context.
Tote initialization image showing sorter position barcode and tote barcode pairing used for rescanning association.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: When a tote is manually removed from the sorter stand, it is essential that the tote with the same Tote ID is returned to its original position.
Proper scanning ensures that the system recognizes the correct placement and avoids operational errors.

The operator opens the application, selects "Sorter Destinations," scans the tote sorter stand position barcode located in the center of the rack on the top rail, and scans the tote barcode for the tote being placed in the stand.
A successful scan pairing causes the scanner to flash green, while an unsuccessful pairing causes a yellow flash and requires rescanning.

missing_details: The source does not provide a detailed operator sequence for recording the original Tote ID before removal.
The source does not specify exact UI field names for the sorter verification beyond Add Tote and Tote ID verification context.
The source does not define a formal escalation path or named downstream team.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not explicitly state whether production stop or LOTO is required for this verification activity.
